flags.concern: Resize preview parity: `computeGroupEdgeResize` is not a straight shared extraction today. The live preview path in `TimelineCanvas` rescales every child in the pinned group, while the commit path in `useClipResize.startGroupEdgeResize()` only mutates the first or last clip. The plan says to share group-edge math but also says to keep child preview projection in `TimelineCanvas`, which would leave preview and commit on different semantics and makes the new helper contract ambiguous.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:681-735` computes group-edge preview by scaling every child override across the whole group; `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts:420-479` commits group-edge resize by updating only `targetClipId`; current tests lock that behavior in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx:334-387` ('mutates last clip only' / 'mutates first clip only').

flags.concern: Verification: the planned `tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` gate is not actionable as written. This repo currently has many unrelated type errors outside `src/tools/video-editor`, so the command does not pass even before this cleanup. Without a narrower config or an explicit filtered-review step, that validation item cannot distinguish a good resize change from pre-existing failures.
flags.evidence: `tsconfig.app.json` includes all of `src` and excludes only tests. Running `./node_modules/.bin/tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` currently exits 2 with unrelated errors in files such as `src/domains/billing/components/CreditsManagement/components/AddCreditsSection.tsx` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/TimelineContainer/TimelineContainer.tsx`.
